Member-only story

Rich-looking people are often broke. The plain-looking neighbor mowing his own lawn is often loaded. Flash and wealth are rarely the same person.

2 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image Created By Ai
• Net Worth Is Invisible
You can’t see a brokerage account from the driveway. You can see a leased SUV. The visible signs of “success” — new cars, designer labels, vacations posted online — are consumption, not wealth. Wealth is what’s not spent.
2. The Millionaire Next Door Profile
Decades of research into self-made millionaires found the same pattern again and again: most live in modest homes, drive older cars, and would never be picked out of a lineup as “rich.” They built wealth precisely by not looking like they had it.
